steps.title: Identify the function
steps.content: Algeria's drop in solid-fuel use is presented as an instance of the kind of shift the "energy ladder" model traditionally explains. The text then uses Heltberg's Ghana study to argue the energy ladder is reductive — fuel use is shaped by many factors beyond income.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content:
Choice D captures this: Algeria illustrates the kind of phenomenon the text goes on to argue is frequently but inadequately explained by the energy ladder. A misframes Algeria as a poor evaluation case, B claims two compelling explanations (the text settles on the multi-factor view), and C suggests Ghana shares the cause (the text actually contrasts Ghana's complexity with the simple ladder model).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
